title: cornus sericea var. asperifolia
intro: Cornus sericea var. asperifolia, also known as Red Osier Dogwood or Red Willow, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, digestive issues, and fever. The health benefits of Cornus sericea var. asperifolia include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and aid in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
